title: MiniMax M2.7 AutoRound XPU allreduce boundary probes

baseline_for_comparison.description: Strict quality-valid no-async path, TP4, XPU graph forced, llm-scaler INT4 MoE, TRITON_ATTN, block-size 256, max_num_batched_tokens 512.

reason_for_probe: Investigate reducing framework-visible collective/callback boundaries by changing how TP allreduce is represented during XPU compilation, without changing model weights, quantization, routing, sampler, KV dtype, or power limits.

candidates.label: custom-allreduce-sp-only
candidates.outcome: The custom allreduce pattern avoided the previous torchbind pattern-construction crash, but stock sequence parallelism failed during Inductor compile with a shape mismatch.
candidates.failure: RuntimeError: The size of tensor a (s72) must match the size of tensor b ((s72//4)) at non-singleton dimension 0.
candidates.interpretation: The stock sequence-parallel rewrite changes the token dimension for reduce-scatter/all-gather, but the MiniMax decode graph still has full-size residual users. It is not safe to enable as-is for this MiniMax TP4 decode path.

candidates.label: custom-allreduce-only
candidates.outcome: The graph compiled and output was deterministic/non-degenerate, but exact token hash changed.
candidates.warning: PyTorch warned that vllm::all_reduce output may alias its input even though the custom op schema says it must not.
candidates.interpretation: A custom-op allreduce representation needs correct mutation/alias semantics, or a real out-of-place runtime implementation, before it can be used for quality-sensitive runs.

candidates.label: functional-out-of-place-allreduce
candidates.outcome: Closer than custom-op allreduce because exact raw canaries passed, but still not promotion-safe because greedy semantic repeats were nondeterministic.
candidates.interpretation: Functional out-of-place allreduce may be useful for future debugging/fusion because it preserves the long raw prompt hashes, but it cannot replace the accepted no-clone c10d path until semantic repeat determinism is fixed.

patch.notes: Focused patch artifact for opt-in XPU allreduce custom-op tracing, functional out-of-place allreduce support, and the sequence-parallel pattern probe support used in these tests.

localmaxxing.reason: No new result passed the full strict quality gate; rejected candidates are recorded locally and in GitHub notes instead.

next_steps: Do not promote stock sequence parallelism for MiniMax TP4 decode without a residual-shape-safe rewrite.
If revisiting custom-op allreduce, first make the operator schema/runtime alias semantics correct and rerun raw plus semantic quality gates.
Investigate why functional out-of-place allreduce is deterministic on raw145 exact prompts but nondeterministic on the multi-prompt semantic repeat suite.
Continue prioritizing true fused allreduce plus Q/K RMS or allreduce plus RMS/MoE epilogue kernels that preserve current accepted math and determinism.
